tasks.title: Define seed_migrations ledger table + generate structural migration
tasks.description: DONE. Table packages/db/src/schemas/seed-migrations/seed_migration.dbschema.ts (name PK varchar255, group varchar20, checksum text, applied_at tstz default now, duration_ms int nullable, result varchar50). Migration 0049_tiny_firebird.sql generated. Decisions: varchar+JSDoc-union not pgEnum for group (matches app_log/audit/cron precedent); result varchar(50) matches billing_dunning_attempts.result; lean append-only table (no BaseModel/soft-delete). Test 15/15 green, @repo/db typecheck clean. Exports: seedMigrations, InsertSeedMigration, SelectSeedMigration.

tasks.title: Add seed_migrations to dbReset preserve-list (with correct table name)
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/utils/dbReset.ts: added 'seed_migrations' to ALWAYS_EXCLUDE_TABLES + JSDoc documenting drizzle_migrations is inert (doesn't match real __drizzle_migrations in drizzle schema; public-scoped scan never finds it; left untouched) vs seed_migrations genuinely needed (in public). Extracted pure partitionTablesForReset({discoveredTables,exclude})->{tablesToReset,tablesSkipped} from resetDatabase for unit-testability. Test 6/6 (pure helper, no DB). T-021 note: resetDatabase calls getDb() singleton so initializeDb() must run first; never call db:seed --reset CLI against shared worktree DB.

Test 8/8 (expectTypeOf + ts-expect-error), typecheck clean. No barrel yet (T-008/T-009 assemble).

tasks.title: Implement ledger read/write layer + checksum
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/ledger.ts: getAppliedMigrations({db})->{names:ReadonlySet, rows}, recordApplied({db,name,group,checksum,durationMs,result}) (single INSERT, tx-safe, no own transaction), computeChecksum({contents})->sha256 hex. Test 8/8 (3 pure + 5 real-DB incl tx-rollback discards ledger row). GOTCHA: test imports seedMigrations from @repo/db/schemas subpath (top barrel resolves undefined under Vitest module runner — see engram testing/repo-db-vitest-barrel-undefined). T-009: call recordApplied with same tx as up(). T-010: reuse recordApplied per file with result='baseline-stamp'.

tasks.title: Implement safeDelete step 1: inbound-FK introspection + active-ref count
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/helpers/fkGuard.ts: getInboundForeignKeys({db,table})->InboundFk[] via pg_constraint + unnest(conkey,confkey) positional (composite-safe discovery, public schema); countActiveReferences({db,table,primaryKeyColumn,primaryKeyValue})->{total, byConstraint[]}. Uses sql.identifier() for dynamic identifiers. Test 4/4 (own pg.Pool+initializeDb bootstrap loading apps/api/.env.local — no seed integration harness exists). T-007 rule: total===0 -> deletable (after operator-edit check); total>0 -> skip, byConstraint gives blocking tables for reason. CAVEAT: composite-FK filter by referencedColumn===pkColumn can overcount genuine composite FKs (none in schema today) — group by constraintName + require ALL pairs if ever targeting composite-keyed table. Self-ref FK not special-cased.

subtasks.title: Query pg catalog for inbound FKs to table
Count active referencing rows for a record

tasks.title: Implement safeDelete step 2: operator-edit detection + skip/warn integration
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/helpers/safeDelete.ts: safeDelete({db, table, where, reason, isOperatorEdited?}) -> SafeDeleteResult. Order: resolve table+single PK (throws composite/missing) -> SELECT target row -> FK guard (countActiveReferences) -> operator-edit guard (optional predicate, default=not-edited with info log) -> DELETE on same db (tx-safe). Contract: where matches 0 rows=no-op success (idempotent); 2+ rows=throws. Predicate receives row keyed by snake_case DB cols (SELECT *). Test 7/7. Drizzle prims: getTableColumns/.primary/.name, getTableName from drizzle-orm. DESIGN: SafeDeleteArgs in types.ts extended with isOperatorEdited? so ctx.helpers.safeDelete carries the predicate uniformly (no direct-import bypass). T-020 billing delete passes Model-C COMMERCIAL_FIELDS-based predicate.

tasks.title: Build data-migration context factory (db/models/services/helpers)
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/context.ts: buildMigrationContext({db, actor?, logger?})->SeedMigrationCtx. models/services derived from barrels via PickByNameSuffix<typeof import(@repo/db),'Model'> / Service mapped type (no hand-enum, 90+/80+ auto). ctx.actor ADDED to contract (services take actor as 1st arg; loadSuperAdminAndGetActor if not injected). helpers.safeDelete closure over passed db (no getDb fallback, tx-safe). Dedup IsOperatorEditedPredicate into types.ts. Test 22/22. T-009: call buildMigrationContext({db:tx, actor}) ONCE PER MIGRATION inside its tx; resolve actor ONCE PER RUN and pass explicitly.

subtasks.title: Unit test: context builds with actor

tasks.title: Implement migration discovery + ledger diff + ordering
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/discover.ts: discoverMigrationFiles({dir?})->DiscoveredMigration[] (fileURLToPath dir resolution, regex ^\d{4}-.+\.(ts|js)$ exclusion, import via pathToFileURL, sorted by numeric prefix; THROWS on duplicate prefix or meta.name!=filename-stem to protect ledger identity); computePendingMigrations({discovered,applied,group?}) pure sync filter preserving order. Test 10/10 (fixtures __fixtures__/discover/). T-009 sequence: discoverMigrationFiles() -> getAppliedMigrations({db}) -> computePendingMigrations(). T-013 make: 4-digit zero-pad NNNN, meta.name MUST equal filename stem, check collision before write.

tasks.title: Implement prod destructive gate + destructive meta flag enforcement
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/prodGate.ts: evaluateProdDataMigrationGate({env, pendingMeta, allowDestructiveFlag}) -> {allowed, reason?, destructiveNames}. Pure, mirrors evaluateProdCleanupGate. Env var HOSPEDA_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE_MIGRATION registered in packages/config/src/env-registry.hospeda.ts (boolean, required:false, default 'false', apps:['seed'], category 'database'); regen json + count 250->251 + snapshot updated. NO Zod schema (cleanup gate reads process.env ad hoc — mirrored). NO Coolify action (default-false refuse-by-default). Test 7/7, config guards 46/46. T-017 wiring: call once pre-run with pendingMeta from T-008 + allowDestructiveFlag=args.includes('--allow-destructive'); abort whole run if !allowed.

tasks.title: Implement runner core (transaction-per-migration, record, abort)
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/runner.ts: runMigrations({db?,group?,allowDestructive?,env?,dir?,actor?,logger?})->{applied[],skipped[],pendingCount}. Flow: resolve db (getDb default) -> discover -> getAppliedMigrations -> computePending -> no-op if empty -> prod gate (abort before running) -> resolve actor ONCE -> per migration db.transaction(tx): buildMigrationContext({db:tx,actor}) -> up(ctx) -> recordApplied({db:tx,...,checksum:readFile}) ; throw rolls back both + aborts run (rethrown with cause). Uses db.transaction() NOT withTransaction (honors injected db). Also exports resolvePendingMigrations({db?,group?,dir?})->{db,discovered,applied,pending} for T-010 reuse. Test 5/5. GOTCHA: ledger.name = full stem WITH numeric prefix (0003-slug), LIKE queries need leading wildcard. T-017: call runMigrations({group,allowDestructive,env:process.env}), omit db+actor; thrown .message is CLI-ready, catch+exit(1).

subtasks.title: Transaction per migration + record on success
Rollback + abort on throw (no ledger row)

tasks.title: Implement baseline-stamp mode
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/baselineStamp.ts: baselineStamp({db?,group?,dir?})->{stamped[]}. Reuses resolvePendingMigrations from runner.ts (unmodified); per pending recordApplied({...,durationMs:0,result:'baseline-stamp'}); up() NEVER called. Idempotent + group filter. Test 3/3 (scratch table stays empty proves no up()). T-019: call baselineStamp({}) as LAST step after full reset+seed, gated to fresh-DB path only (idempotent but don't stamp a non-fresh DB). T-017: --baseline-stamp flag calls baselineStamp instead of runMigrations (no actor/gate/env args).

subtasks.title: Unit test: zero pending after stamp

tasks.title: Implement status reporter (applied vs pending)
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/status.ts: computeMigrationStatus (pure), getMigrationStatus({db?,group?,dir?}) (I/O), formatMigrationStatus (pure renderer). MigrationStatus={applied[{name,group,appliedAt,result,orphaned}],pending[{name,group}],counts}. Orphan handling: ledger row whose file is gone stays in applied flagged orphaned:true. toSeedMigrationGroup() narrows varchar group defensively. Test 11/11. T-017: db:seed:migrate:status calls getMigrationStatus({group}) then console.log(formatMigrationStatus(status)).

tasks.title: Implement db:seed:make scaffold generator
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/data-migrations/make.ts: makeMigration({slug, group?='required', destructive?=false, dir?})->{filePath,name}. normalizeSlug: trim+lowercase then ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$ (starts with letter so never looks like prefix), throws on invalid. Prefix = max existing NNNN +1 zero-pad4 (gap-safe). Template imports types from ./types.js, meta.name=exact stem, up(ctx) stub + dual-write TODO. Test 18/18. T-017: import from ../data-migrations/make.js (no barrel), parse --group/--destructive, print filePath. NOTE: uppercase input silently lowercased, camelCase NOT auto-kebabbed (single-word passes, multiword fails) — CLI help should say so.

tasks.title: Implement deterministic UUIDv5 scheme for example fixtures (OQ-1)
tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/src/utils/deterministicFixtureId.ts: deterministicFixtureId({seedKey}) -> UUIDv5 via node:crypto SHA-1 (RFC 4122 §4.3) — NO uuid dep added (not in repo). SEED_FIXTURE_NAMESPACE='b17f196e-df93-4151-8e23-71487d77e4fc' (hardcoded, must never change). Exported from utils/index.ts barrel. Test 6/6 with pinned known-answer vectors. Seed-key convention for T-015/T-016: '<entity>:<fixture.id>' (prefix with entity/folder name to avoid cross-entity id collision).

tasks.title: Add explicit-id passthrough to the seed create path
tasks.description: DONE. CRITICAL FINDING: service.create() CANNOT honor incoming id — all @repo/schemas create schemas .omit({id:true}) and Zod strips unmodeled keys. Determinism must go model-direct (bypass service) OR change schemas. T-015 implemented model-direct capability: SeedFactoryConfig.deterministicId?:{modelClass,getId} opt-in in seedFactory.ts — when getId(item) returns a value, bypasses service.create() and calls new modelClass().create({...data,id,createdById,updatedById}); else byte-for-byte unchanged (defaultRandom). Test 16/16. TRADE-OFF for T-016 (OWNER DECISION PENDING): model-direct bypasses service lifecycle (_canCreate, service normalizers.create, _before/_afterCreate hooks e.g. slug gen) per entity — vs schema-level additive optional id (preserves lifecycle but touches @repo/schemas SSOT + exposes id to API/admin callers). T-016 blocked on this decision.

Unit tests: explicit vs default id

Model-direct wired for: accommodations (AccommodationModel, kept fixture slug instead of stripping), accommodation_faqs (AccommodationFaqModel, seedKey accommodationFaq:<accSeedKey>:<index>), events (EventModel, kept slug). Exported helpers getAccommodationFixtureId/getAccommodationFaqFixtureId/getEventFixtureId. Test 48/48. FLAGGED-AND-SKIPPED (hooks compute DB-state-dependent values, not pure per-fixture — replicating = business-logic dup/risk): destinations (_beforeCreate computes slug/level/path/pathIds from parent DB row = hierarchy math), accommodation_reviews (_afterCreate recalcs parent accommodation aggregate stats via @internal method), destination_faqs/reviews (parents not wired), + other entities not touched (experiences, gastronomies, posts, tags, event child tables). RULE: pure-per-fixture hook -> replicate; cross-row/DB-dependent -> flag-skip. Follow-up tracked separately (T-025). OWNER DECISION 2026-07-07: model-direct not schema-level.

tasks.description: DONE. Both db:fresh + db:fresh-dev are shell chains in root package.json. Appended `&& pnpm --filter @repo/seed seed --data-migrate --baseline-stamp` after the baseline seed (db:fresh: at end; db:fresh-dev: after main seed, before db:seed:test-users). Test scripts/__tests__/db-fresh-baseline-stamp.test.ts (3/3) asserts wiring (stamp appears once, after seed, before test-users). Needs explicit ops decision → T-023 must document the manual stamp step for prod day-1.

tasks.description: DONE. packages/seed/test/data-migrations/lifecycle.integration.test.ts 4/4: (1) pending->run->applied->no-op cross-checked runner.applied vs getMigrationStatus vs getAppliedMigrations field-by-field; (2) mid-batch abort — 2nd of 3 throws, status shows 1 applied + thrower&successor pending, scratch confirms rollback; (3) baselineStamp result='baseline-stamp' durationMs=0, scratch empty (no up()); (4) real-dir discover finds ported 0001-0003, discovery<->status agree. tasks.description: DONE 6/6. packages/seed/test/integration/hos25-t022-reseed-determinism.integration.test.ts. Per-mechanism: posts (top-level deterministicId factory), accommodation_faqs (child composite seed-key), destinations (hierarchy — level/path/pathIds/parentDestinationId reconstruct identical across 2 runs), accommodation_reviews (recalculateStats aggregate correct + id stable), gastronomies (raw-Drizzle bypassed), cross-entity id isolation. Method: withRealDbTransaction insert->assert persisted id==deterministicFixtureId->DELETE->reinsert->assert identical->rollback (no global reset). All persisted ids matched expected + stable across reseed. tasks.title: CI drift guard: seed data change without a data-migration
tasks._done: scripts/check-seed-dual-write.sh (bash, decide() split for test-injection via CHANGED_FILES_OVERRIDE/MARKER_TEXT_OVERRIDE, fails-open on unresolvable BASE_SHA). Guards: data/{amenity,attraction,destination,exchangeRate*,feature,revalidationConfig,sponsorship*,postTag}/**, data/user/required/**, data/tag/{internal,system}-*.json (MIXED folder, prefix-guarded), billing/src/config/{plans,limits,entitlements,addons,promo-codes}.config.ts. New-migration = git-added NNNN-*.ts. Opt-out marker [skip-seed-migration]: <reason> in PR body/commit. Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml guards job (fetch-depth:0 + env-passed BASE_SHA/PR_BODY). Test 10/10 + shellcheck clean. No npm alias (matches check-schema-drift.sh precedent). KNOWN GAP v1: inline-TS-constant seeders (rolePermissions/aiPrompts/aiSettings/socialAutomation/contentModeration/systemUser) NOT covered — future tightening.
tasks.description: Enforce the dual-write rule (spec R-5) in CI. Design a guard script (e.g. scripts/check-seed-dual-write.sh or a vitest guard) that inspects the PR diff: if it modifies BASELINE seed DATA (packages/seed/src/data/**/*.json for required catalog rows, and the billing plan/limit/entitlement TS constants) WITHOUT adding a new packages/seed/src/data-migrations/NNNN-*.ts file in the same diff, FAIL with a clear message pointing at `pnpm db:seed:make`. FIRST propose the detection approach (diff-based file-pair heuristic — which data paths count, how to detect 'a new migration was added', how to allow an explicit opt-out label/marker for legit data changes that genuinely need no migration e.g. a bulk additive column) and its false-positive/negative profile, THEN implement. Wire into CI (.github/workflows) alongside the existing schema drift guard. Tests: the guard script's own logic (given a synthetic changed-files list -> pass/fail). NOTE owner-approved 2026-07-07 (dual-write enforcement = rule + CI check).

subtasks.title: Opt-out mechanism for legit no-migration data changes

tasks.title: Complete example determinism: destinations + reviews + remaining entities
tasks.description: DONE (destinations+reviews+event-children; experiences/gastronomies/posts split to T-026). Destinations+FAQs FULLY wired — fixtures do NOT carry level/path/pathIds (seed CLAUDE.md WRONG, correct in T-023); exported PURE hierarchy helpers (computeHierarchyLevel/Path/PathIds/isValidParentChildRelation) from service-core + reused in destinations.seed.ts preProcess (in-memory hierarchyBySeedId cache, root-first so no DB roundtrip). accommodation_reviews+destination_reviews wired: added minimal public recalculateStats(id,ctx?) on both review services (wraps existing private aggregation; @internal-style no-actor/no-Result matching updateStatsFromReview precedent — accepted), seed calls it per row in postProcess. eventLocations/eventOrganizers wired. Tests: destinations.seed.test.ts 17, determinism.test extended, service-core recalc integration test. 987 seed pass. DEFERRED to T-026: experiences/gastronomies/posts (large relation-builder work); tags N/A (empty manifest). ORIG: Owner chose FULL example determinism (2026-07-07). INVESTIGATE-THEN-IMPLEMENT, ROBUST over quick (owner pref): (1) destinations — fixtures ALREADY carry level/path/pathIds/destinationType (per seed CLAUDE.md), so pass them through model-direct + resolve parentDestinationId via idMapper; VERIFY the _beforeCreate hierarchy math (computeHierarchyLevel/Path/PathIds) isn't adding anything the fixture lacks — do NOT re-implement it if the fixture already has correct values. (2) accommodation_reviews — _afterCreate recalcs parent accommodation aggregate stats via @internal recalculateAndUpdateAccommodationStats; do NOT hand-duplicate the aggregation — EXPOSE a public recalc method on AccommodationService (e.g. recalculateStats({accommodationId})) and call it from the seed postProcess after model-direct inserts. Same pattern for destination_reviews if applicable. If exposing a service-core API is non-trivial or has wider blast radius, REPORT before committing. Use deterministicFixtureId({seedKey:'<entity>:<fixture.id>'}) with distinct prefixes. Tests: reseed-stability per entity + verify parent aggregates correct after review inserts. Scope note: if too large, prioritize destinations+accommodation_reviews and list what's deferred.

tasks.description: Finish the FULL example determinism owner decision (T-025 deferred these 3 large entities to avoid half-wiring). Wire deterministic model-direct ids (deterministicId opt-in, deterministicFixtureId({seedKey:'<entity>:<fixture.id>'}) distinct prefixes) for experiences, gastronomies, posts example seeders. SAME audit-first rule as T-016/T-025: for each, check the service create hooks (_beforeCreate/_afterCreate/normalizers.create) — pure-per-fixture (e.g. slug from static fields) -> replicate/passthrough; cross-row/DB-dependent (aggregates, hierarchy) -> reuse/expose a service API like T-025 did for reviews (do NOT hand-duplicate), or if truly non-trivial REPORT before committing. tags is N/A (empty manifest — confirm + note). Also handle any child tables (gastronomy faqs/reviews, post relations). Tests: per-entity reseed-stability (getId + real reseed) + any parent-aggregate correctness. Scoped verify @repo/seed + @repo/service-core if touched.
